description_long: This object is a historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four solid wooden wheels. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and made of metal, featuring a slightly flared muzzle and reinforcing rings along its length. The carriage is constructed from rough, dark wood planks, reinforced with metal brackets and bolts, and includes two metal rings on each side, likely for maneuvering or securing the cannon. Several spherical cannonballs are placed near the carriage, indicating its function as an artillery piece. The overall appearance is weathered, with visible wood grain and a matte, slightly rough metal finish. This type of object would have been used in historical urban fortifications or during sieges.
description: A historical metal cannon on a wooden wheeled carriage, accompanied by several cannonballs. The object has a weathered, rugged appearance with visible wood grain and matte metal.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the cannon barrel, the wooden carriage, and a cluster of cannonballs beside the wheels.
description_view_1: Front view down the barrel, highlighting the circular muzzle opening and the arrangement of cannonballs to the left.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the cannon barrel, carriage, and wheels, with no cannonballs visible on this side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the carriage, the tailspike, and cannonballs grouped to the right.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
